dimension: Self-hosting Cohere Command A+ (CohereLabs/command-a-plus-05-2026, Apache 2.0) as the "Mirror" calibration/citation auditor in POLARIS's clinical RAG pipeline — parameter count, VRAM/GPU footprint, vLLM/SGLang serving support, the JSON-vs-citation structured-output tradeoff, and OpenAI-compatible HTTP serving.
key_findings: PARAMETER COUNT (corrects the premise): Command A+ is NOT the same 111B dense model as Command A. It is a 218B-total / 25B-active-per-token SPARSE MIXTURE-OF-EXPERTS (MoE) transformer. Architecture: 'Decoder-only Sparse Mixture-of-Experts Transformer Model' with '128 experts, out of which 8 are active per token, and a single shared expert is applied to all tokens.' Attention interleaves sliding-window-attention layers (RoPE) with global-attention layers (no positional embeddings) in a 3:1 ratio. Context: 128K input / 64K output. The bf16 card states '219B total parameters' (the headline figure rounds to 218B; expert-count rounding accounts for the 218 vs 219 discrepancy).

VRAM AT BF16: 219B params x 2 bytes = ~438 GB of weights alone (plus KV cache + activations for 128K context). Official model-card requirement: 8 x H100 (640 GB total) on Hopper, OR 4 x B200 (768 GB) on Blackwell. There is NO single- or quad-H100 bf16 option; ~438 GB of weights exceeds 4x80=320 GB and is too tight on 6x80=480 GB once KV cache is added, hence the 8xH100 floor.

VRAM AT W4A4: Official requirement is 2 x H100 (160 GB total) on Hopper, OR 1 x B200 (192 GB) on Blackwell. The HF artifact is CohereLabs/command-a-plus-05-2026-w4a4. CRITICAL nuance: w4a4 is NVFP4 (4-bit weights + 4-bit activations, two-level scaling) applied to the MoE experts ONLY — 'The attention path, i.e., Q/K/V/O projections, the KV cache, and attention compute, is kept at full precision' and uses Quantization-Aware Distillation. So it is NOT a flat 218B x 0.5 bytes = ~109 GB; the full-precision attention/shared/embedding tensors push the real footprint above 109 GB, which is why it needs 2xH100 (160 GB) rather than 1xH100 (80 GB). Treat ~109 GB as a hard floor and ~110-150 GB as the working envelope that fits inside the official 160 GB (2xH100) ceiling.

A100 IS NOT A VIABLE w4a4 TARGET (and the official table never lists A100). NVFP4/FP8 are not natively supported on Ampere (A100) — they are emulated via higher-precision kernels with no latency or memory-bandwidth advantage over bf16; the A100's optimized path is FP16/BF16. The official GPU table only offers Hopper (H100) and Blackwell (B200). So for the 4-bit deployment the realistic 80GB-class options are 2xH100 or 1xB200; A100 would gain nothing from the NVFP4 quant and is effectively unsupported for w4a4.

vLLM SUPPORTS Command A+ / Cohere2 (Command-4) architecture. Requires vllm >= 0.21.0 plus transformers and cohere_melody >= 0.9.0 (the melody library is needed for accurate response/citation parsing). Launch flags: --tool-call-parser cohere_command4 --reasoning-parser cohere_command4 --enable-auto-tool-choice. Example (w4a4 on 1xB200, set -tp 2 for 2xH100): 'vllm serve CohereLabs/command-a-plus-05-2026-w4a4 -tp 1 --tool-call-parser cohere_command4 --reasoning-parser cohere_command4 --enable-auto-tool-choice'.

SGLANG support is PARTIAL/EMERGING and should be treated as lower-confidence. The w4a4 and fp8 HF model cards DO include an SGLang launch command ('python3 -m sglang.launch_server --model-path CohereLabs/command-a-plus-05-2026-w4a4 --host 0.0.0.0 --port 30000'), but the broader Cohere2ForCausalLM architecture support in SGLang traces to an open feature request (sgl-project/sglang#4570) and the unusual 3:1 sliding-window/global-attention pattern. vLLM is the production-grade, fully-documented path; SGLang is offered but the Command-4 parser tooling (cohere_command4 parsers, cohere_melody) is documented only for vLLM. Recommend vLLM for the Mirror role.

STRUCTURED-OUTPUT vs CITATION TRADEOFF — CONFIRMED, with an important self-hosting distinction. (a) On Cohere's HOSTED Chat API: structured outputs (response_format json_object AND json_schema) are documented as NOT supported in RAG mode — 'This feature is currently not supported in RAG mode.' response_format is unsupported whenever documents/connectors/tools/tool_results are provided. So on the managed API, native grounded citations and strict JSON-schema output are mutually exclusive. (b) On the SELF-HOSTED model (vLLM): citations are emitted as INLINE TEXT SPANS, not JSON — the model uses paired <co>...</co: tool_id:[result_idx]> tags, e.g. 'the total sales amount was <co>10000</co: 0:[0]>'. Grounding is enabled via enable_citations=True in tokenizer.apply_chat_template. So self-hosted citation output is parseable but is a custom span syntax, NOT a JSON citations array. (c) Cohere's hosted API DOES return a structured message.citations array (start/end/text/sources) — but that's the managed-API convenience layer, not what vLLM emits raw.

OPENAI-COMPATIBLE SERVING: YES. vLLM exposes an OpenAI-compatible HTTP server via 'vllm serve' implementing /v1/chat/completions and /v1/completions. Clients connect by overriding base_url, e.g. OpenAI(base_url='http://localhost:8000/v1', api_key='token-abc123'). The Command A+ model cards explicitly show 'curl -X POST http://localhost:8000/v1/chat/completions'. POLARIS's OpenRouterClient-style HTTP path will work against a self-hosted vLLM server with a base_url override; the Cohere-specific tool-call/reasoning parsers run server-side and do not change the OpenAI wire protocol. SGLang's server (port 30000) is likewise OpenAI-compatible at /v1/chat/completions.

load_bearing_claims.claim: Command A+ is 218B-total / 25B-active MoE (128 experts, 8 active + 1 shared), NOT a 111B dense model like the original Command A.
load_bearing_claims.why_load_bearing: The user's premise asked whether A+ equals the 111B Command A. The MoE answer changes everything downstream: VRAM math, why w4a4 only quantizes experts, and why it needs multiple GPUs. If POLARIS sized hardware assuming a 111B dense model, it would under-provision.

load_bearing_claims.claim: w4a4 requires 2 x H100 (160 GB) or 1 x B200 (192 GB); A100 is NOT a viable 4-bit target because NVFP4/FP8 are merely emulated on Ampere with no benefit.
load_bearing_claims.why_load_bearing: This is the actual deployment-sizing decision for the Mirror role. The user explicitly asked 'how many 80GB GPUs (A100/H100).' The answer is 2xH100 minimum — and the A100 caveat prevents POLARIS from buying/renting A100s expecting the 4-bit speedup.

load_bearing_claims.claim: w4a4 quantizes MoE experts ONLY; the attention path stays full precision, so the footprint is above the naive 218B x 0.5 = ~109 GB and needs 2xH100, not 1xH100.
load_bearing_claims.why_load_bearing: If someone computes VRAM as 218B x 0.5 bytes = 109 GB they would wrongly conclude a single 80GB H100 suffices. The selective-quantization fact is the reason a single H100 fails and 2xH100 is the floor.

load_bearing_claims.claim: Self-hosted Command A+ emits citations as inline <co>...</co: 0:[0]> text spans, not as a JSON array; and Cohere's hosted API forbids response_format (JSON schema/object) in RAG mode.
load_bearing_claims.why_load_bearing: The Mirror role needs a parseable calibration verdict. This means POLARIS CANNOT get both native grounded citations AND a strict JSON schema in one call on the managed API; on self-host it must parse the custom <co> span syntax rather than rely on JSON-schema-constrained decoding for the citation portion. Drives the Mirror output-contract design.

load_bearing_claims.claim: vLLM (>=0.21.0, + transformers + cohere_melody>=0.9.0, with cohere_command4 tool/reasoning parsers) serves Command A+ behind an OpenAI-compatible /v1/chat/completions endpoint reachable via base_url override.
load_bearing_claims.why_load_bearing: Confirms POLARIS's existing OpenRouterClient-style HTTP path can drive the self-hosted Mirror with only a base_url change — no new client. vLLM (not SGLang) is the supported production path; the melody library + command4 parsers are the dependency POLARIS must install for correct citation/reasoning parsing.

recommendation: For POLARIS's Mirror role, self-host CohereLabs/command-a-plus-05-2026-w4a4 on 2 x H100 (160 GB) via vLLM >= 0.21.0 (with transformers + cohere_melody >= 0.9.0, flags --tool-call-parser cohere_command4 --reasoning-parser cohere_command4 --enable-auto-tool-choice -tp 2), and connect POLARIS's OpenRouterClient-style HTTP path with base_url -> http://<host>:8000/v1. Do NOT plan on A100s for the 4-bit quant (NVFP4 is emulated, no benefit). For the Mirror calibration verdict: design the output contract around the inline <co>...</co: tool_id:[idx]> span syntax (enable_citations=True), NOT a JSON-schema citations array — native grounded citations and strict JSON structured output are mutually exclusive on Cohere's stack. Two viable Mirror patterns: (1) two-pass — first call produces grounded answer + <co> spans, parse them with cohere_melody, then a second non-grounded call emits the JSON calibration verdict; or (2) prompt the model to wrap its calibration verdict as JSON in the free-text response and parse it (no schema enforcement). Prefer vLLM over SGLang (SGLang Cohere2 support is partial / open issue #4570). Treat the SGLang status and the exact w4a4 GB (109 GB floor; ~110-150 GB working) as the only medium-confidence items.
sources: https://huggingface.co/CohereLabs/command-a-plus-05-2026 (model card: 218B total / 25B active MoE, 128 experts/8 active+1 shared, 3:1 SWA:global attention, 128K/64K context, Apache 2.0, GPU table BF16=4xB200/8xH100, FP8=2xB200/4xH100, W4A4=1xB200/2xH100, inline <co> citation spans)

https://huggingface.co/CohereLabs/command-a-plus-05-2026-w4a4 (w4a4: 1xB200/2xH100, NVFP4 4-bit weights+activations on MoE experts only, attention path full precision, QAD, vllm>=0.21.0 + cohere_melody>=0.9.0, SGLang launch command present, enable_citations=True via apply_chat_template)
https://huggingface.co/CohereLabs/command-a-plus-05-2026-fp8 (fp8: 2xB200/4xH100; both vLLM and SGLang OpenAI-compatible /v1/chat/completions launch commands)

https://docs.cohere.com/docs/structured-outputs and https://docs.cohere.com/v1/docs/structured-outputs ('This feature is currently not supported in RAG mode.' — response_format json_object/json_schema unsupported when documents/connectors/tools/tool_results provided)
https://docs.cohere.com/docs/rag-citations (hosted API returns structured message.citations array with start/end/text/sources; accurate vs fast citation modes)

https://docs.vllm.ai/en/stable/serving/openai_compatible_server/ (vllm serve = OpenAI-compatible HTTP server, /v1/chat/completions + /v1/completions, base_url override e.g. OpenAI(base_url='http://localhost:8000/v1'))
https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang/issues/4570 (open feature request: Support Cohere Command-A / Cohere2ForCausalLM in SGLang — partial support)

https://www.edge-ai-vision.com/2025/10/nvidia-blackwell-the-impact-of-nvfp4-for-llm-inference/ and NVIDIA Hopper docs (NVFP4/FP8 emulated on Ampere/A100 with no benefit; native on Hopper FP8 / Blackwell NVFP4)

dimension: Self-hosting IBM Granite Guardian 4.1 8B as a RAG hallucination detector (Sentinel role): native output contract, risk dimensions, VRAM, serving, and per-claim call convention
key_findings: OUTPUT CONTRACT (confirms Codex): Granite Guardian 4.1 emits a BINARY yes/no inside <score> tags, NOT JSON and NOT span attribution. Exact strings: '<score>yes</score>' or '<score>no</score>'. In think mode a <think>...reasoning...</think> block precedes the score; in no-think mode it emits empty '<think>\n</think>' then the score. Canonical parser strips <think> then regex-extracts <score>: re.findall(r"<score>\s*(.*?)\s*</score>", text_clean, re.DOTALL); returns 'yes'/'no' lowercased. NO calibrated risk probability / logit / logprobs is documented on the 4.1 model card (unlike the loose 3.x folklore — even 3.3's card produces categorical scores, not softmax-over-yes/no on its card). SCORING SEMANTICS for groundedness: 'yes' = risk DETECTED (ungrounded/hallucinated), 'no' = grounded/faithful. This polarity is critical for the SentinelVerdict mapping.

RISK DIMENSIONS: Pre-baked criteria in 3 buckets. HARM: Social Bias, Jailbreaking, Violence, Profanity, Unethical Behavior. RAG HALLUCINATION (this is the relevant bucket): (a) Groundedness = text faithfulness to provided documents (THE RAG faithfulness check), (b) Context Relevance = retrieved-doc relevance to the query, (c) Answer Relevance = response adequacy to the query. AGENTIC: Function-Calling Hallucination. Plus BYOC (Bring Your Own Criteria) free-text custom criteria — strengthened in 4.1. YES, there is a dedicated groundedness check directly usable for RAG faithfulness, and a separate context-relevance check.

GROUNDEDNESS IS THE RIGHT CRITERION for per-claim (claim, cited-span) faithfulness. Built-in groundedness criteria text (verbatim): 'A text is considered ungrounded or unfaithful if it includes information lacking support from, or directly contradicting, the provided document(s). This risk arises when the text fabricates details, misinterprets the content, or makes unsupported extrapolations beyond what is explicitly stated in the document(s).' For POLARIS per-claim use: put the CLAIM as an assistant-role message, put the cited span as documents=[{'doc_id':'0','text':<span>}], pass the groundedness criteria via build_guardian_block. score=='yes' => UNSUPPORTED/FABRICATED; score=='no' => grounded.

EXACT GROUNDEDNESS CALL (verbatim from 4.1 card): documents=[{'doc_id':'0','text':context_text}]; messages=[{'role':'assistant','content':response_text},{'role':'user','content':build_guardian_block(criteria, think=think)}]; prompt=tokenizer.apply_chat_template(messages, tokenize=False, add_generation_prompt=True, documents=documents). Function-calling variant adds user_text first message and passes available_tools=tools instead of documents=. think=True selects GUARDIAN_JUDGE_THINK instruction (step-by-step reasoning trace then score); think=False selects GUARDIAN_JUDGE_NOTHINK (immediate score, empty think tags). No-think is preferred on the low-latency hot path for yes/no gating.

VRAM / HARDWARE: granite-guardian-4.1-8b is fine-tuned from granite-4.1-8b, which is a DENSE decoder-only transformer (GQA, RoPE, SwiGLU MLP, RMSNorm; 40 layers, 4096 emb, 32 attn heads / 8 KV heads, 12800 MLP hidden) — NOT a Mamba-2 hybrid, so no exotic engine requirement. 8B params @ bf16 = ~16GB weights; with KV-cache/activations ~24-32GB practical. Comfortably fits 1xA100 40GB and 80GB. On RTX 4090 24GB: bf16 is tight/borderline; quantized fits with large headroom (base granite-4.1-8b GGUF Q4_K_M ~5.35GB; IQ4_XS 4.83GB, Q4_K_S 5.09GB) so a 4090 24GB easily serves a 4-bit Guardian. Tensor type bf16, Apache-2.0, base release April 2026.

SERVING: Granite 4.1 family is officially optimized for vLLM, SGLang, and llama.cpp. The model card's own example uses vLLM (from vllm import LLM, SamplingParams; LLM(model=model_path, max_model_len=8192); SamplingParams(temperature=0.0, max_tokens=2048)). vLLM exposes an OpenAI-compatible server (vllm serve) generically — but NOTE: the guardian <guardian>-block prompt assembly relies on the model's HF chat template (apply_chat_template with documents=/available_tools=); to use vLLM's OpenAI /v1/chat/completions you must either rely on vLLM applying the same chat template server-side or pre-render the prompt and use the completions endpoint. Cleanest for Sentinel: vLLM offline LLM.generate() with a pre-rendered prompt, temperature=0.0. No specific minimum vLLM/SGLang version is pinned on the cards (use latest). Context window 8K tokens (max_model_len=8192).

CONTEXT LIMIT: Guardian 4.1 documented/served at 8K tokens (max_model_len=8192) — even though the base granite-4.1-8b supports up to 131072. For per-claim (claim, cited-span) checks the 8K window is ample; do NOT feed whole corpora — one claim + its cited span(s) per call.

PERFORMANCE (from IBM): RAG groundedness Average BAcc ~0.76 in thinking mode; agentic-workflow hallucination BAcc 0.74->0.79 in no-think. These are balanced-accuracy detector metrics, not faithfulness guarantees — Sentinel should treat a 'yes' as a flag for escalation/down-grade, not an infallible verdict.

load_bearing_claims.claim: Granite Guardian 4.1 native output is a binary yes/no inside <score></score> tags — NOT JSON, NOT span attribution, and no exposed risk probability/logprob on the 4.1 model card.
load_bearing_claims.why_load_bearing: This is THE determinant of the SentinelVerdict schema. The schema must be {risk_detected: bool (from yes/no), criterion: str, optional reasoning: str (from <think>), no native confidence float, no native span}. Any span attribution or per-token confidence must be supplied by POLARIS's own pre-segmentation (one claim + cited span per call), NOT extracted from the model output. Designing the schema to expect a probability or span would be unimplementable against the real model.

load_bearing_claims.claim: For groundedness, score=='yes' means UNGROUNDED/HALLUCINATED (risk detected) and 'no' means grounded/faithful.
load_bearing_claims.why_load_bearing: Polarity inversion. If POLARIS maps 'yes' to 'verified/faithful' the entire hallucination gate is backwards — fabrications would pass and grounded claims would be flagged. Lethal in clinical context per CLAUDE.md §-1.1.

load_bearing_claims.claim: 4.1 dropped guardian_config={'criteria_id':...} (the 3.x API) and instead embeds a free-text <guardian> block (build_guardian_block) as the last user message, with text-to-judge as an assistant message and RAG context via documents=.
load_bearing_claims.why_load_bearing: Determines the exact prompt-assembly code the Sentinel wrapper must implement. Code copied from 3.x cookbooks (criteria_id) will silently mis-prompt 4.1. The claim/span placement (assistant=claim, documents=span, user=<guardian> block) is the correct per-claim convention.

load_bearing_claims.claim: granite-4.1-8b is a dense decoder-only transformer (not Mamba-2 hybrid); 8B bf16 ~16GB weights, ~24-32GB practical, fits 1xA100 40/80GB; tight at bf16 on a 24GB 4090 but trivial at 4-bit (~5GB).
load_bearing_claims.why_load_bearing: Drives the deployment/hardware decision and whether quantization is required for a consumer GPU. The hybrid-vs-dense distinction also affects engine-version assumptions; being dense means no special vLLM/SGLang build is needed.

load_bearing_claims.claim: Guardian 4.1 served at 8K context (max_model_len=8192); per-claim (claim + cited span) calls fit easily but whole-corpus feeding does not.
load_bearing_claims.why_load_bearing: Constrains the Sentinel call granularity. The right design is one call per (claim, cited-span) pair, which matches POLARIS's existing per-sentence provenance-token model and stays well under 8K.

dimension: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B as POLARIS Judge-role terminal arbiter (structured verdict emission) + Vast.ai as self-hosted serving substrate
key_findings: PART A.1 — SLUG: OpenRouter live model id is `qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b` (confirmed present in the `supported_parameters=structured_outputs` filter list). HF repo is `Qwen/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B`, Apache 2.0, MoE 35B total / 3B active, 256 experts (8 routed + 1 shared), 262K native context (1M via YaRN). NOTE the runtime lock file `config/architecture/polaris_runtime_lock.yaml` line 77 records `qwen/qwen-3.6-35b-a3b` (extra hyphen before 3.6) — that string is NOT the OpenRouter id and would fail a PG_JUDGE_MODEL call. Reporting, not fixing (the file is a Codex-APPROVE + operator-signature-locked artifact; mutation is out of scope for research).

PART A.1 — VRAM (corrected): Because it is MoE, all 35B params must be VRAM-resident even though only ~3B activate per token (sparsity buys speed, not memory). bf16 weights ≈ 70 GB → tight/impractical on a single 80GB H100 once 262K-context KV cache is added; needs an H200 (141GB) or 2x80GB / TP. FP8 checkpoint `Qwen/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8` ≈ 35 GB → fits cleanly on one H100 80GB or H200 (recommended self-host target; H100+ has FP8 tensor cores, A100 does NOT — use INT8 on A100). INT4/GPTQ ≈ 21 GB → fits a 24GB GPU (4090) for the Judge's small structured-classification workload.

PART A.2 — STRUCTURED VERDICT: Yes, strongly supported. The fixed 5-value enum {VERIFIED|PARTIAL|UNSUPPORTED|FABRICATED|UNREACHABLE} maps directly to vLLM `guided_choice: [...]` (exact one-of-N guarantee) — the cleanest fit for a terminal arbiter. Also supports `response_format={type:'json_schema', json_schema:{...}}` with an enum-typed field (via xgrammar default backend in 2026, or outlines/lm-format-enforcer/guidance). Native function-calling/tool-use enabled by serving with `--enable-auto-tool-choice --tool-call-parser qwen3_coder --reasoning-parser qwen3` (vLLM>=0.19.0 / sglang>=0.5.10). Model is thinking-mode-by-default with <think> blocks (no /nothink soft switch in 3.6) — the reasoning-parser separates the trace from the final structured verdict.

PART A.2 — DELIVERY-PATH RISK (ties A to B): OpenRouter's own docs warn structured outputs are provider-dependent and it 'might fall back to json_object' silently; only `provider.require_parameters=true` forces routing to providers that honor json_schema. A terminal clinical arbiter silently dropping from a strict enum to free-form JSON is exactly the silent-degradation LAW/§9.1 forbids. Self-hosted vLLM `guided_choice` is a HARD guarantee with no fallback — this is the integrity argument for Vast.ai self-hosting, on top of sovereignty.

PART B.3 — VAST.AI vLLM SETUP: Yes. Official Vast.ai vLLM template uses the `vllm/vllm-openai:latest` Docker image (OpenAI-compatible server on port 8000). Flow: create/edit template → set `VLLM_MODEL` (HF repo, e.g. `Qwen/Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-FP8`) and `VLLM_ARGS` (vllm serve flags: tensor-parallel-size, max-model-len, reasoning/tool-call parsers) → filter offers for sufficient VRAM+disk → Rent → instance auto-launches `vllm serve`. Endpoint: `https://INSTANCE_IP:EXTERNAL_PORT/v1/...` (self-signed cert → `curl -k`), auth via `Authorization: Bearer <OPEN_BUTTON_TOKEN>`. CLI: `vastai create instance <id> --image vllm/vllm-openai:latest --env '-p 8000:8000' --disk <GB> --args --model <repo>`.

PART B.4 — REPRODUCIBILITY / INTERRUPTION: Use ON-DEMAND, not interruptible. On-demand = fixed host price, exclusive GPU control for the instance lifetime, cannot be interrupted by other users (only ends on your stop or host non-renewal). Interruptible = bid-based; paused mid-run if outbid OR if any on-demand renter claims the same resource ('on-demand always takes precedence'). For a clinical benchmark that must complete uninterrupted, on-demand is the only safe choice.

PART B.4 — RESIDUAL RISK MITIGATION: Vast is a decentralized marketplace (tier-4 datacenters down to hobbyists), so even on-demand can drop if a host disconnects. Mitigate by filtering on (a) verified-datacenter hosts (blue 'verified' label; criteria: pro-managed DC, >=5 flagship GPUs A100/H100/H200, registered business) and (b) reliability score >0.95 (0-1 from historical uptime/interruption frequency). Vast explicitly states no built-in uptime SLA/autoscaling/monitoring — add your own health-check + idempotent re-run on the runner side.

PART B.5 — COLD-START: It is a ONE-TIME provisioning cost, not per-question — reframe accordingly. First boot pulls the vLLM image + downloads weights from HF (~35GB FP8 / ~70GB bf16) + loads + CUDA-graph capture. Empirical rough order: 7B(~14GB)=5-10min, 70B(~140GB)=20+min, so FP8 35B-A3B lands ~10-20 min depending on host network. Architect the smoke to provision ONCE and keep the server warm for the whole benchmark (per-question latency is then just inference). Speed-ups available: pre-download to instance-local disk via huggingface-cli + pass local path, store FP8 checkpoint, fastsafetensors/run-ai load_format (cited ~70% cold-start reduction).

load_bearing_claims.claim: The correct OpenRouter id is `qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b` (no hyphen before 3.6); the runtime lock file line 77 has `qwen/qwen-3.6-35b-a3b` with an extra hyphen.
load_bearing_claims.why_load_bearing: If the typo'd slug is used as PG_JUDGE_MODEL, every Judge call fails / the pathB architecture-coverage gate cannot observe the role. The lock file is operator-signed; fixing requires a Codex-APPROVE'd brief + operator commit (mutation policy in the file header), so it must be raised as a lock-mutation Issue, not silently edited.

load_bearing_claims.claim: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B is MoE — all 35B params are VRAM-resident; only ~3B activate per token. bf16 ≈ 70GB (not the ~54GB that one search returned, which was the 27B dense variant).
load_bearing_claims.why_load_bearing: Drives Vast.ai instance sizing. The wrong (54GB) figure would suggest one 80GB H100 is comfortable; the correct ~70GB makes bf16 tight-to-OOM at 262K context. Self-hosting should target the FP8 checkpoint (~35GB) on one H100/H200, or accept TP/multi-GPU for bf16.

load_bearing_claims.claim: vLLM `guided_choice` gives a HARD one-of-N enum guarantee for the verdict; OpenRouter's hosted structured-outputs can silently fall back to json_object unless `provider.require_parameters=true` is set.
load_bearing_claims.why_load_bearing: For a clinical terminal arbiter, a silent drop from strict enum to free-form JSON is the §9.1 silent-degradation failure mode — an unparseable or out-of-enum verdict could let a fabrication through. This is the core integrity reason to self-host the Judge (or, if using OpenRouter, to force require_parameters).

load_bearing_claims.claim: On-demand Vast.ai instances cannot be interrupted by other users for the instance lifetime; interruptible (spot) instances are paused by a higher bid OR any on-demand claim on the same resource.
load_bearing_claims.why_load_bearing: A reproducible clinical benchmark must not be paused mid-run. Choosing interruptible to save cost would risk a corrupted/partial benchmark; on-demand + verified-DC host + reliability>0.95 is the correct reproducibility posture.

load_bearing_claims.claim: Cold-start (image pull + ~35-70GB weight download + load) is a one-time provisioning cost, not a per-question cost, if the server is provisioned once and kept warm for the whole benchmark.
load_bearing_claims.why_load_bearing: The question framed cold-start as 'matters for a per-question smoke.' If the smoke is architected to re-provision per question it would be 10-20+ min/question and infeasible; the fix is architectural (warm server), not a model/provider choice.

dimension: Sovereignty implications of serving POLARIS clinical LLM inference on Vast.ai rented GPUs (narrow threat model: no US company in the runtime LLM-inference path + no clinical data in US jurisdiction)
key_findings: VERDICT: Vast.ai-EU is sovereign-INFERIOR to a direct-EU provider for the clinical inference path. Recommendation: keep the canonical direct-EU plan (OVH/Scaleway/Hetzner per sovereign_gpu_capacity_confirm.md) for clinical inference; Vast.ai-EU's legitimate role is the NON-clinical FP4/vLLM serving-stack de-risk spike (the ~$400 spot-GPU step already scoped in that doc), where no real clinical query flows. It is acceptable-with-mitigations for the demo only if a direct-EU box cannot be procured in the window, and even then it is strictly second-best.

Vast.ai Inc. is a US legal entity: California stock corporation (filed 2018-08-23, Los Angeles) that is a branch of a Delaware parent. It operates the marketplace CONTROL PLANE (payments, rental contract, scheduling, SOC2 Type II) and brokers GPUs from independent third-party hosts ranging from Tier-4 datacenters down to home hobbyists. Sources: OpenCorporates CA C4187907, Crunchbase, Vast.ai security FAQ.

Q1 (does clinical data touch US jurisdiction when running your own vLLM container on an EU-located GPU?): YES, residually — through TWO mechanisms, NOT through GPU physical location. (a) The GPU physically sits in the EU = good for data-residency. (b) BUT the host operator retains root on the physical machine and a privileged Vast.ai management daemon runs ON that host; clinical queries are decrypted into host RAM + GPU VRAM during the vLLM forward pass, where host root can read them (CUDA VRAM is not reliably zeroed; the host owns the NVIDIA driver). Encryption-at-rest does NOT protect in-flight inference. (c) Because a US-incorporated broker has privileged daemon access to the host, data is arguably within Vast.ai's 'possession, custody, or control' and thus reachable under US legal process (CLOUD Act) — that is the prong that touches US jurisdiction.

The SSH proxy-vs-direct question is the WEAKEST part of the concern, not the strongest: Vast.ai's default proxy SSH routes traffic through Vast.ai (US) servers, but direct SSH + POLARIS's own TLS to the vLLM endpoint defeats that path entirely. The IRREDUCIBLE exposures are host-root in-RAM/VRAM access and the privileged US-broker daemon on the box — neither is fixed by choosing direct SSH.

Q2 (is EU-GPU-via-US-marketplace materially different from direct-EU?): YES, but the gap is narrowable to a single residual. Direct-EU (OVH/Scaleway/Hetzner bare-metal or dedicated) gives a clean chain where the ONLY root-holder is your contractual EU data processor/controller — exactly the 'Vendor is the data controller' attestation sovereign_gpu_capacity_confirm.md §3 compliance-ask #3 is built on. On Vast.ai, filtering to verified ISO-27001 EU datacenter partners (Secure Cloud) collapses the physical/host-security delta toward direct-EU parity. After that filter, the irreducible residual delta is EXACTLY ONE thing: the US broker (Vast.ai Inc.) interposed in the control plane with privileged host access = CLOUD Act reachability. That single residual is precisely what the narrow threat model forbids ('no US company anywhere in the runtime LLM inference path').

Q4 concrete EU-only filter — use an ALLOWLIST, not a US denylist (notin:['US'] still admits CN/RU/etc.). Recommended search-offers JSON: {"geolocation": {"in": ["FR","DE","NL","FI","IE","SE"]}, "verified": {"eq": true}, "datacenter": {"eq": true}, "static_ip": {"eq": true}}. geolocation uses ISO-2 country codes; verified+datacenter restricts to vetted Secure Cloud partners; static_ip enables a stable direct (non-proxy) endpoint.

CAVEAT on the filter (residual the filter CANNOT close): geolocation pins where the MACHINE sits, not the host operator's nationality/incorporation — there is NO host-domicile filter, so a US-domiciled host with an EU machine passes. Reported geolocation is also self-attested and weaker for unverified hosts (another reason to require verified+datacenter). 'Non-Five-Eyes-if-stricter' would additionally drop CA/GB/AU/NZ — but note POLARIS's OWN posture is non-US and explicitly Canada-OK (Vexxhost Montréal, OVH BHS), so a Five-Eyes exclusion is STRICTER than the project's own model and would drop the Canada the operator otherwise prefers. Surface this as an operator tradeoff, do not silently apply it.

load_bearing_claims.claim: During vLLM inference the clinical query is decrypted into host RAM and GPU VRAM, where the physical host's root user can read it; encryption-at-rest does not cover this and CUDA VRAM is not reliably zeroed.
load_bearing_claims.why_load_bearing: This is the decisive technical fact that defeats any 'mitigations make Vast.ai fine' conclusion. If false (e.g., confidential-computing/TEE GPUs that wall off host root), the recommendation would flip toward Vast.ai-EU being acceptable. It rests on standard unprivileged-container semantics (host kernel/root sees container memory) plus Vast.ai's own FAQ admitting host operators are in the trust boundary, not on a Vast.ai statement that VRAM is exposed.

load_bearing_claims.claim: Vast.ai Inc. is US-incorporated (CA stock corp / DE parent) and runs a privileged management daemon on each host plus the marketplace control plane, making host-resident clinical data reachable under US legal process (CLOUD Act).
load_bearing_claims.why_load_bearing: This is the prong that puts a 'US company in the runtime inference path' and 'US jurisdiction over clinical data' — the exact two things the narrow threat model forbids. The CLOUD Act reach turns on 'possession, custody, or control'; the privileged-daemon-on-host fact is what supplies the 'control' nexus. If Vast.ai were EU-incorporated or had zero host-resident privileged software, this prong would weaken substantially.

load_bearing_claims.claim: Filtering to verified EU datacenter partners (Secure Cloud) narrows the host/physical-security risk toward direct-EU parity, leaving the US-broker control-plane interposition as the single irreducible residual.
load_bearing_claims.why_load_bearing: This is the crux of the Q2 'materially different?' answer and of the recommendation's nuance. It is what lets the analysis say 'acceptable-with-mitigations for a fallback' rather than 'categorically forbidden,' while still concluding 'strictly second-best.' If the residual were larger (multiple irreducible gaps) the call would be a hard no; if zero, Vast.ai-EU would equal direct-EU.

load_bearing_claims.claim: geolocation filters machine location (ISO-2), not host operator domicile, and is self-attested; there is no host-nationality filter.
load_bearing_claims.why_load_bearing: Determines the honest ceiling of the Q4 concrete filter. If the operator believes the filter guarantees a non-US host entity, they would over-trust it. The filter guarantees data-residency (machine in EU) but not host-entity nationality — a material gap for a sovereignty posture that cares about the operator's jurisdiction, not just the rack location.

recommendation: Prefer the canonical direct-EU provider (OVH France / Scaleway / Hetzner per docs/sovereign_gpu_capacity_confirm.md) for the clinical LLM inference path. It preserves the clean single-root-holder, 'Vendor is the data controller' chain (§3 compliance-ask #3) that the existing sovereign procurement plan and transparency.md are built on, with zero US entity in the runtime inference path. Do NOT route real clinical queries through Vast.ai for the Carney demo. Vast.ai-EU's legitimate, sovereign-acceptable use is the NON-clinical FP4/vLLM serving-stack de-risk spike already scoped in sovereign_gpu_capacity_confirm.md §2 (the ~$400 spot-GPU step) — synthetic/benchmark prompts only, no clinical data, so the US-broker residual is moot there. If, and only if, no direct-EU box can be procured in the demo window and Vast.ai becomes a forced fallback: restrict to verified EU datacenter partners (geolocation allowlist + verified + datacenter + static_ip), use direct SSH + POLARIS's own TLS to the vLLM endpoint + full-disk encryption, and DISCLOSE in transparency.md that a US-incorporated broker (Vast.ai Inc.) is interposed in the control plane with privileged host access — a residual CLOUD-Act exposure that does not exist with direct-EU. Residual-risk caveat that survives ALL mitigations: the clinical query is exposed in host RAM/GPU VRAM to the physical host's root and to a US-broker daemon during inference; encryption-at-rest and SSH choice do not close this. That is why Vast.ai-EU is strictly second-best for clinical inference. (Per POLARIS MEMORY.md, route this recommendation through Codex CLI for the binding review when carried back into the main POLARIS loop; advisor was used here only because this research harness mandates it.)

https://docs.vast.ai/guides/reference/faq/security (container isolation, verified vs unverified hosts, TLS, encrypt-at-rest is user responsibility)

dimension: Release policy for non-VERIFIED Judge verdicts in a clinical-grade deep-research tool (clinical-safety + competitive-quality)
recommendation: REJECT one-size-fits-all (options a/b/c/d as a single choice). The highest-quality policy is VERDICT-SPECIFIC and TWO-AXIS, mirroring the already-frozen, Codex-approved verdict-severity ordering that POLARIS's scoring layer locked (reconcile.py: VERIFIED<PARTIAL<UNREACHABLE<UNSUPPORTED<FABRICATED, conservative-MAX; claim_audit_scorer.py: FABRICATED+UNSUPPORTED=hard_fail, PARTIAL=0.5, UNREACHABLE full-weight-but-not-hard-fail, S3 observe-only). The release policy must reuse this ordering, not invent a parallel one. Split the decision into (i) claim-action and (ii) report-gate (release_allowed). FABRICATED(material) = occurrence-gate (refuse-in-place AND release_allowed=false even after removal — fabrication is an active falsehood + generator-integrity signal). UNSUPPORTED(material) = residual-gate (rewrite one pass; if still unsupported, refuse-in-place; release_allowed=false only if post-removal Lane-2 material coverage drops below the locked 0.70 threshold). PARTIAL / UNREACHABLE = soft (caveat-and-keep with visible verdict marker, release_allowed stays true). Gate on ANY material (S0-S2) hard-fail with ZERO tolerance — never a count/rate threshold (that is §-1.1-banned metadata AND resurrects the BUG-M-205 "after three failures" anti-pattern the code already fixed). The competitive differentiator is the per-claim verdict ledger + transparent machine-checkable gap-list, NOT blanket suppression ("we only show verified claims" is the WRONG framing — the literature recommends systematic verification over abstention). Drop never means silent deletion: refuse-in-place per the atom_refusal_validator STRICT precedent + gaps.json sidecar (LAW II fail-loudly). release_allowed=false/abort still emits report.md as a reviewable verdict artifact (§9.1 invariant 4) — it means "gated from auto-release pending human sign-off," not "no output."
key_findings: RECOMMENDED VERDICT->ACTION TABLE (two-axis; applies to MATERIAL S0-S2 claims only; S3 = observe/log-only, never gates, per _MATERIAL_SEVERITIES + scorer observed_S3_excluded):

VERIFIED -> claim-action: keep in body prose as-is, no marker needed -> release_allowed: TRUE (unaffected) -> manifest: success / pass (gate_class=pass). This is the only verdict that enters body prose without a caveat.

PARTIAL -> claim-action: KEEP with a visible inline verdict marker ('partially supported: <what the span does/does not establish>') -> release_allowed: TRUE -> manifest: partial_evaluator_advisory if PARTIAL share is high enough to be material to coverage, else pass. Rationale: scorer weights PARTIAL 0.5 (soft, not hard_fail); suppressing it would drop true-but-incomplete clinical nuance (e.g. 'effect shown in deficient populations only'). Option (b) rewrite is OPTIONAL here, not required.

UNREACHABLE -> claim-action: KEEP with visible marker stating the unreachable SUBTYPE (paywall | robots | fetch_failure | source_missing) -> release_allowed: TRUE -> manifest: partial_evaluator_advisory. Rationale: scorer counts UNREACHABLE full-weight-bad for ranking but it is NOT a hard_fail and NOT a fabrication — the claim may be true but the evidence chain is incomplete, which is exactly the transparency signal frontier tools hide. SUBTYPE-CONDITION: source_missing (cited source does not exist) is a citation-fabrication smell -> escalate to UNSUPPORTED handling; paywall/robots/fetch_failure stay soft.

UNSUPPORTED (material) -> claim-action: REWRITE + RE-VERIFY ONE PASS (option b, bounded, reusing the existing §9.2 section-level 'attempt one regeneration' budget — do NOT double-regenerate); if still UNSUPPORTED, REFUSE-IN-PLACE (sentence replaced with refusal marker per atom_refusal_validator STRICT layer) + gaps.json entry -> release_allowed: RESIDUAL-GATE = FALSE only if post-removal Lane-2 material coverage < 0.70 (the locked _COVERAGE_THRESHOLD); otherwise TRUE -> manifest: partial_evaluator_advisory (coverage holds) or abort_no_verified_sections / abort_evaluator_critical (coverage collapses). Rationale: an unsupported claim is a missing evidence link, not necessarily a lie — give it one honest rewrite, then refuse loudly.

FABRICATED (material) -> claim-action: REFUSE-IN-PLACE immediately (NO rewrite pass — you do not 'retry' a fabrication) + gaps.json entry recording the refuting/absent span -> release_allowed: OCCURRENCE-GATE = FALSE even though the claim is removed -> manifest: abort_evaluator_critical (reuse existing status, do NOT invent). Rationale: FABRICATED is an active falsehood AND a generator-integrity red flag; conservative-MAX 'a problem either auditor finds counts' + §-1.1 'patients can be hurt by a wrong dose/contraindication' mandate gating the whole run for human sign-off when ANY material fabrication occurred, not just scrubbing the sentence and shipping.

AXIS DISTINCTION (the core design decision): FABRICATED uses OCCURRENCE-gating (the run is held because a fabrication HAPPENED, regardless of cleanup) while UNSUPPORTED uses RESIDUAL-gating (the run is held only if what REMAINS after cleanup is too thin to be useful). This split is what makes the table engineered rather than asserted, and it unifies cleanly with the scorer's two-lane design (Lane-1 faithfulness hard_fail vs Lane-2 coverage threshold).

ZERO-TOLERANCE, NOT COUNT-THRESHOLD: gate on ANY single material (S0-S2) FABRICATED, or any material UNSUPPORTED surviving the one rewrite. NEVER 'release_allowed=false after N failures' or 'if unsupported_rate > x' — count/rate gating is the §-1.1-BANNED metadata pattern and is the exact BUG-M-205 'after three total failures' bug that evaluator_gate.py already removed.

'HIGHEST QUALITY' DEFINITION (sub-Q1 tradeoff): for a clinical DR tool, highest quality = MINIMIZE materially false claims that reach the reader as if verified (false-claim minimization dominates), SUBJECT TO maintaining a useful coverage floor (Lane-2 >= 0.70) and MAXIMIZING transparency (every non-verified claim visible as a typed verdict + gap, never silently dropped). Coverage and length are explicitly SUBORDINATE — per memory feedback_frontier_dr_not_agentic, length is liability not advantage in clinical context; auditable per-sentence faithfulness is the deliverable.

COMPETITIVE FRAMING (sub-Q3): frontier DR tools (ChatGPT 5.5 Pro / Gemini 3.1 Pro DR) emit unverified AND fabricated content INDISTINGUISHABLE from verified prose (web evidence: 3-18% medical fabrication, hallucinated-URL 3-13%, the documented 15-minutes-to-find fabrication because surrounding text was accurate; ECRI 2026 ranks AI-chatbot misuse the #1 health-tech hazard). They effectively run policy (d) 'allow, no gate.' POLARIS's differentiator is therefore NOT 'we only show verified claims' (blanket suppression sacrifices transparency + coverage and the literature recommends verification-over-abstention) but 'every claim carries a machine-checkable terminal verdict, non-verified claims are refused-in-place with a typed gap-list, and a single material fabrication gates the run for human sign-off.'

MANIFEST/release_allowed COMPOSITION: this claim-level policy layers ON TOP of the existing evaluator_gate.py report-level gate (which already sets release_allowed=false on judge-critical axes / deterministic PT08/PT11/PT12 blockers). Reuse the existing taxonomy — pass / partial_evaluator_advisory / abort_evaluator_critical / abort_no_verified_sections — and the existing release_allowed boolean; do NOT add a new status. Aggregate per-claim verdicts into the manifest so release_allowed = (no material FABRICATED occurred) AND (no material UNSUPPORTED survived rewrite that drops coverage below 0.70) AND (existing report-level gate passes).

load_bearing_claims.claim: POLARIS's benchmark scorer already locks the relative severity of the five verdicts (reconcile.py _VERDICT_ORDER: VERIFIED<PARTIAL<UNREACHABLE<UNSUPPORTED<FABRICATED; claim_audit_scorer.py: hard_fail = FABRICATED + UNSUPPORTED, PARTIAL weight 0.5, UNREACHABLE full-weight-but-not-hard-fail, S3 observe-only).
load_bearing_claims.why_load_bearing: This is the coherence anchor. If the release policy used a different ordering than the frozen, Codex-approved, pre-registered scoring policy, POLARIS would have two contradictory internal opinions on verdict severity — a self-inconsistency a clinical reviewer (or Codex) would immediately flag. The entire verdict-specific recommendation derives its defensibility from mirroring this locked ordering.

load_bearing_claims.claim: The locked Lane-2 coverage threshold is 0.70 (_COVERAGE_THRESHOLD) and material severity = S0-S2 only (S3 observe-only), both frozen in claim_audit_scorer.py / reconcile.py.
load_bearing_claims.why_load_bearing: The UNSUPPORTED residual-gate ('release_allowed=false only if post-removal coverage < 0.70') and the S0-S2-only gating scope are quantitatively pinned to these exact frozen values. If either changed, the recommended release_allowed effect would change. Using any other number would be an invented threshold violating LAW VI / the pre-registration property.

load_bearing_claims.claim: evaluator_gate.py already implements release_allowed + gate_class (pass/partial/abort) and the BUG-M-205 fix specifically removed count-threshold gating ('after three total failures') in favor of per-rule zero-tolerance blocking.
load_bearing_claims.why_load_bearing: The recommendation to compose-not-duplicate (reuse partial_evaluator_advisory / abort_evaluator_critical) and to forbid count/rate thresholds rests on this. Recommending a new status or a rate-gate would reintroduce the exact anti-pattern the code already fixed and create a §-1.1-banned metadata check.

load_bearing_claims.claim: atom_refusal_validator.py establishes refuse-in-place (sentence replaced with refusal marker) + gaps.json sidecar as the existing precedent for non-passing claims; it does NOT silently delete.
load_bearing_claims.why_load_bearing: The 'drop never means silent deletion' instruction for FABRICATED/UNSUPPORTED is grounded in this precedent. Silent deletion would violate LAW II (fail loudly) and discard the transparency that is POLARIS's competitive differentiator. The recommendation's 'refuse-in-place' verb is the existing code's verb, not a new invention.

load_bearing_claims.claim: Frontier DR tools surface unverified/fabricated content indistinguishable from verified prose (web evidence: 3-18% medical fabrication rates, ECRI 2026 #1 health-tech hazard), and the literature recommends systematic per-claim verification over abstention.
load_bearing_claims.why_load_bearing: This drives sub-Q3 and the rejection of 'we only show verified claims (blanket suppression)' as the differentiator. If frontier tools already gated or the literature favored abstention, the recommended transparent-gap-list-plus-occurrence-gate framing would lose its competitive justification.
